title: ocotea mollifolia
intro: Ocotea mollifolia, also known as the 'Palo Santo' or 'Blessed Wood', is a type of tropical evergreen tree native to South America. The medicinal properties of Ocotea mollifolia are attributed to its essential oils, which contain compounds like limonene and beta-caryophyllene, known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ocotea mollifolia include reducing stress and anxiety, alleviating digestive issues, and promoting relaxation. Ocotea mollifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, essential oils, and incense, which are used in traditional medicine and spiritual practices for their calming and purifying effects.
